incorrects.description: Student selected Table A, Daily Steps with values 4500, 3200, 5100, 2800

steps.text: Look at the numbers in Table A. They all end in hundreds: 4,500, 3,200, 5,100, 2,800. You could use Scale of 5 or even larger scales. Which table has numbers that don't end in 0 or 5?

incorrects.description: Student selected Table C, Books Read with values 25, 30, 35, 40

steps.text: Table C has 25, 30, 35, 40. Every number ends in 0 or 5. You could use Scale of 5 or Scale of 10 for those. Which table has numbers that aren't multiples of 5?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Table B highlights and its values pulse: 7, 12, 19, 23.

steps.text: Here's how to think about it. Look at Table B: 7, 12, 19, 23. None of these end in 0 or 5. That means you can't use Scale of 5 or Scale of 10. Scale of 2 is the only way to show every number exactly. Table B has to use Scale of 2.
